category_title: Students with ASD who exhibit school avoidance or distress during schedule changes
scenario_title: Following multi-step directions with varying teacher expectations
minimal_context: The tutor is asking the student to complete a multi-step science assignment in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please take out your science binder, turn to the lab section, complete the three questions on the board, and then bring your paper to my desk.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: My other teacher doesn't make us do all that. He is very laid back. You are very structured.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: This is what we need to do right now. Please get started.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't have my binder organized. I don't know where the lab section is.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just look through your papers and find it.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (starts flapping hands) It's too many steps! I can't remember what to do after I find the paper. I don't know how to start this!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please take out your science binder, turn to the lab section, complete the three questions on the board, and then bring your paper to my desk.

STUDENT: My other teacher doesn't make us do all that. He is very laid back. You are very structured.

TUTOR: This is what we need to do right now. Please get started.

STUDENT: I don't have my binder organized. I don't know where the lab section is.

TUTOR: Just look through your papers and find it.

STUDENT: (starts flapping hands) It's too many steps! I can't remember what to do after I find the paper. I don't know how to start this!

task.content: Executing academic and daily tasks requiring executive function, such as following multiple step directions, organizing materials, and self-initiating work

context.environment: high school classrooms with varying instructional styles
context.social_setting: dealing with so many different personalities with teachers: some teachers are very laid back and some teachers are very structured

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: inconsistencies across teachers in their implementation of IEP goals
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: difficult for students with ASD to meet expectations, greater difficulties with organization, following multiple step directions, and the ability to self-initiate
related_quotes: the consistency is sometimes difficult because you’re dealing with so many different personalities with teachers: some teachers are very laid back and some teachers are very structured, and it’s really hard to get all that consistency sometimes.

behavior_patterns.condition: participating in the functional demands of high school
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: struggle with executive functions more than their peers
